text.title: Mistake Memory Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Mistake Memory application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Mistake MUST be written within 30 min of detection; later writeback rejected.
rule.rationale: Memory written hours later is reconstructive.

rule.statement: Root-cause chain MUST be ≥3 levels of Why; shallower analysis rejected.
Prevention step MUST be concrete ("add lint rule X", "add CI check Y"); generic preventions ("be careful") rejected.
rule.rationale: Generic preventions are wishes, not rules.

rule.statement: Relevant mistakes MUST be injected into agent context before similar tasks.
Second occurrence of the same mistake MUST trigger an automatic CI rule.
rule.rationale: Second occurrence is the signal that human discipline failed.
